raw_output:
{
  "risk_tier": "High",
  "risk_summary": "A fully automated ML-driven credit decisioning system operating across all 50 US states and the UK processes sensitive financial and behavioural data with no human review, creating high-severity exposure under federal credit reporting law, multiple state privacy frameworks, and financial data security obligations.",
  "applicable_frameworks": [
    {
      "name": "Fair Credit Reporting Act / Regulation V",
      "abbr": "FCRA",
      "jurisdiction": "US Federal",
      "why_applicable": "The system uses third-party data broker inputs and ML models to make credit decisions for personal loans, directly triggering FCRA obligations including risk-based pricing notices and adverse action requirements."
    },
    {
      "name": "Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "abbr": "GLBA",
      "jurisdiction": "US Federal",
      "why_applicable": "The deployment is a financial institution offering personal loans and handling nonpublic personal financial information, making it subject to the FTC Safeguards Rule's information security program requirements."
    },
    {
      "name": "FTC Act Section 5",
      "abbr": "FTC Act §5",
      "jurisdiction": "US Federal",
      "why_applicable": "Fully automated credit decisions using behavioural and third-party data without human review could constitute unfair or deceptive acts or practices, particularly regarding undisclosed data uses and model bias."
    },
    {
      "name": "New Jersey Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "NJDPA",
      "jurisdiction": "US State — New Jersey",
      "why_applicable": "The system makes automated credit decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects for NJ residents and processes sensitive financial and behavioural data, triggering NJDPA profiling and data protection obligations."
    },
    {
      "name": "Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "MN MCDPA",
      "jurisdiction": "US State — Minnesota",
      "why_applicable": "Credit decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects and profiling of Minnesota residents based on financial and behavioural data fall within the MN MCDPA's scope."
    },
    {
      "name": "Maryland Online Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "MODPA",
      "jurisdiction": "US State — Maryland",
      "why_applicable": "The processing of personal data for profiling in credit decisioning presents heightened risk of harm and requires documented data protection assessments per MODPA, effective October 2025."
    },
    {
      "name": "Connecticut Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "CTDPA",
      "jurisdiction": "US State — Connecticut",
      "why_applicable": "Automated credit decisions constitute decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects for Connecticut residents, engaging CTDPA rights including opt-out of profiling and human review requirements."
    }
  ],
  "gaps": [
    {
      "title": "No Human Review of Automated Credit Decisions",
      "regulation": "New Jersey Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "NJDPA",
      "article": "C.56:8-166 (profiling definition and consumer rights regarding automated decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects)",
      "description": "NJDPA defines 'profiling' as automated processing to evaluate economic situation, reliability, or behaviour, and recognises credit and lending decisions as producing legal or similarly significant effects. The deployment makes fully automated decisions with no human review, denying consumers the ability to meaningfully contest or obtain human reconsideration of adverse credit decisions.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "January 15, 2025"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Human Review of Automated Credit Decisions — Minnesota",
      "regulation": "Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "MN MCDPA",
      "article": "325M.11(i) and 325M.11(s) (decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects; profiling)",
      "description": "MN MCDPA identifies denial of financial or lending services as a decision producing legal or similarly significant effects and defines profiling as automated processing of personal data to evaluate economic situation or behaviour. Fully automated decisions without human review capability likely violate consumer rights to opt out of or contest such profiling.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "July 31, 2025"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Human Review of Automated Credit Decisions — Connecticut",
      "regulation": "Connecticut Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "CTDPA",
      "article": "Section 1(12) and Section 4 (decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects; consumer right to opt out of profiling)",
      "description": "CTDPA defines credit and lending decisions as producing legal or similarly significant effects and grants consumers rights regarding profiling used to make such decisions. The deployment's fully automated model with no human review pathway fails to provide the required opt-out or human reconsideration mechanism.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "July 1, 2023 (already in force)"
    },
    {
      "title": "Failure to Provide Risk-Based Pricing Notices",
      "regulation": "Fair Credit Reporting Act / Regulation V",
      "abbr": "FCRA",
      "article": "FCRA §615(h) / Regulation V — Risk-Based Pricing Rule",
      "description": "The system uses third-party data broker inputs (consumer report data) and ML-derived credit scores to set loan terms. Where terms offered are materially less favourable than those offered to a substantial proportion of consumers, a risk-based pricing notice must be delivered. No such notice mechanism is described in the deployment, creating a per-decision statutory violation.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "Already in force"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Adverse Action Notice Mechanism",
      "regulation": "Fair Credit Reporting Act / Regulation V",
      "abbr": "FCRA",
      "article": "FCRA §615(a) / Regulation V",
      "description": "Where a consumer report is used and credit is denied or offered on less favourable terms, the FCRA requires an adverse action notice disclosing the consumer reporting agency used, the consumer's right to a free report, and the right to dispute. Fully automated denials with no described adverse action notification process violate this requirement.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "Already in force"
    },
    {
      "title": "Absence of Written Information Security Program",
      "regulation": "Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "abbr": "GLBA",
      "article": "16 C.F.R. §314.4 (nine required elements of information security program)",
      "description": "As a financial institution processing nonpublic personal financial information for personal loans, the deployment must maintain a written information security program covering risk assessment, access controls, encryption, multi-factor authentication, penetration testing, incident response, and annual board reporting. No such program is described in the deployment.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "Already in force (amended Rule effective June 2023; breach notification effective May 2024)"
    },
    {
      "title": "No FTC Breach Notification Procedure",
      "regulation": "Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "abbr": "GLBA",
      "article": "16 C.F.R. §314.4(j) (breach notification within 30 days for notification events affecting 500+ consumers)",
      "description": "The Safeguards Rule requires financial institutions to notify the FTC no later than 30 days after discovering a security breach involving unauthorised acquisition of unencrypted information for 500 or more consumers. No breach notification process is described for the deployment.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "May 2024 (already in force)"
    },
    {
      "title": "Missing Data Protection Assessments for High-Risk Profiling",
      "regulation": "Maryland Online Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "MODPA",
      "article": "MODPA §14-4610(A)(4) and §14-4610(B) (data protection assessments for profiling presenting heightened risk, including per-algorithm assessments)",
      "description": "MODPA requires controllers to conduct and document regular data protection assessments for each algorithm used in profiling that presents a reasonably foreseeable risk of financial injury, disparate impact, or unfair treatment. Automated ML-driven credit decisioning clearly meets this threshold and no assessment process is described.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "October 1, 2025"
    },
    {
      "title": "Inadequate Privacy Notice Disclosing Third-Party Data Broker Use",
      "regulation": "New Jersey Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "NJDPA",
      "article": "C.56:8-166.6(a)(3) and (4) (privacy notice must identify categories of third parties and personal data shared)",
      "description": "The deployment uses third-party data broker inputs in its credit model. NJDPA requires a clear privacy notice identifying categories of third parties to whom personal data is disclosed and the categories of personal data shared. Use of data brokers in credit scoring must be explicitly disclosed.",
      "severity": "Medium",
      "deadline": "January 15, 2025"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Opt-Out Mechanism for Sale/Sharing of Personal Data for Profiling",
      "regulation": "New Jersey Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "NJDPA",
      "article": "C.56:8-166.6(b) (controller must disclose and provide opt-out for profiling in furtherance of decisions producing significant effects)",
      "description": "Where personal data is processed for profiling in furtherance of decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects, NJDPA requires the controller to disclose this and provide a clear opt-out mechanism. The deployment provides no described opt-out pathway for NJ residents.",
      "severity": "Medium",
      "deadline": "January 15, 2025"
    },
    {
      "title": "FTC Act Unfairness Risk from Fully Automated Decisioning Without Bias Controls",
      "regulation": "FTC Act Section 5",
      "abbr": "FTC Act §5",
      "jurisdiction": "US Federal",
      "article": "FTC Act §5 (unfair or deceptive acts or practices)",
      "description": "The FTC has indicated that use of algorithmic models incorporating behavioural and third-party data in credit decisions, without adequate bias testing or human oversight, may constitute an unfair practice causing substantial injury to consumers who cannot reasonably avoid harm and for which there is no countervailing benefit. No bias audit, fairness testing, or consumer safeguard process is described.",
      "severity": "High",
      "deadline": "Already in force"
    },
    {
      "title": "Processor Contract Requirements for Third-Party Data Brokers",
      "regulation": "Maryland Online Data Privacy Act",
      "abbr": "MODPA",
      "article": "MODPA §14-4609(A) (third-party data use inconsistent with collection promises; controller obligations over processors)",
      "description": "MODPA requires that third parties using consumer information inconsistent with promises made at collection notify affected consumers. The deployment ingests third-party data broker inputs without described contractual controls ensuring those data brokers obtained data lawfully and consistently with disclosed purposes.",
      "severity": "Medium",
      "deadline": "October 1, 2025"
    }
  ],
  "recommended_actions": [
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "action": "Implement an adverse action notice process for all automated credit denials or unfavourable decisions, disclosing the consumer reporting agencies used, consumers' right to a free report, and the right to dispute inaccuracies, as required for each decision where third-party data broker inputs are used.",
      "regulation": "Fair Credit Reporting Act / Regulation V",
      "timeframe": "immediate"
    },
    {
      "priority": 2,
      "action": "Design and deploy a risk-based pricing notice mechanism, either through direct comparison of terms offered or using the credit score proxy method (cutoff score), to notify consumers who receive materially less favourable loan terms than the best terms available.",
      "regulation": "Fair Credit Reporting Act / Regulation V",
      "timeframe": "immediate"
    },
    {
      "priority": 3,
      "action": "Develop and implement a written information security program covering all nine elements under 16 C.F.R. §314.4, including designating a Qualified Individual, conducting a written risk assessment, implementing MFA, encrypting customer data in transit and at rest, conducting annual penetration testing and biannual vulnerability scans, and creating an incident response plan.",
      "regulation": "Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "timeframe": "immediate"
    },
    {
      "priority": 4,
      "action": "Establish an FTC breach notification procedure capable of reporting qualifying security events (500+ consumers, unencrypted data) within 30 days of discovery using the FTC's online reporting form.",
      "regulation": "Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "timeframe": "30 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 5,
      "action": "Introduce a meaningful human review pathway for adverse automated credit decisions, enabling consumers in Connecticut, New Jersey, Minnesota, and Maryland to request reconsideration by a human decision-maker, and document this process in consumer-facing disclosures.",
      "regulation": "Connecticut Data Privacy Act; New Jersey Data Privacy Act; Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act",
      "timeframe": "30 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 6,
      "action": "Conduct and document an algorithmic impact and bias assessment of the ML credit model, testing for disparate impact across protected characteristics, and establish an ongoing fairness monitoring programme to mitigate FTC Act §5 unfairness risk.",
      "regulation": "FTC Act Section 5",
      "timeframe": "30 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 7,
      "action": "Update the consumer-facing privacy notice to disclose: (a) categories of personal data processed; (b) all third-party data broker categories used; (c) profiling and automated decisioning practices; (d) consumers' opt-out rights for profiling in credit decisions; and (e) how to exercise rights and appeal decisions — ensuring compliance across NJ, CT, MN, and MD requirements.",
      "regulation": "New Jersey Data Privacy Act; Connecticut Data Privacy Act; Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act",
      "timeframe": "30 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 8,
      "action": "Commission and document a data protection assessment for each algorithm used in the credit decisioning pipeline, weighing benefits against risks of financial injury, disparate impact, and unfair treatment, and establish a schedule for regular reassessment — ready for MODPA enforcement from October 2025.",
      "regulation": "Maryland Online Data Privacy Act",
      "timeframe": "90 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 9,
      "action": "Audit all third-party data broker agreements to ensure data was collected with appropriate consumer disclosures, that processor contracts include security and purpose-limitation obligations, and that data use is consistent with promises made to consumers at collection.",
      "regulation": "Maryland Online Data Privacy Act; Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act / FTC Safeguards Rule",
      "timeframe": "90 days"
    },
    {
      "priority": 10,
      "action": "Prior to UK launch, conduct a UK-specific regulatory gap analysis covering UK GDPR Article 22 (automated decision-making rights), ICO guidance on credit scoring, FCA Consumer Duty, and the Consumer Credit Act 1974 — this analysis falls outside the retrieved clauses but is critical before go-live.",
      "regulation": "UK regulatory frameworks (pre-launch gap analysis)",
      "timeframe": "90 days"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "This fully automated ML credit decisioning deployment faces critical compliance gaps under FCRA (adverse action and risk-based pricing notices), the GLBA Safeguards Rule (written information security program and breach notification), FTC Act §5 (algorithmic unfairness
parse_error: Unterminated string starting at: line 222 column 14 (char 16155)
